name: Arrays
description: Linear sequences. Foundation for almost every other DSA topic — sorting, two-pointer scans, prefix sums.

name: Strings
description: Character sequences. Heavy overlap with Arrays plus parsing, palindromes, and pattern matching.

name: Hash Maps
description: Constant-time lookup. The single most useful data structure for interview problems.

name: Linked Lists
description: Pointer manipulation, cycle detection, in-place reversal.

name: Stacks & Queues
description: LIFO/FIFO structures. Powers monotonic stack problems and BFS traversals.

name: Trees
description: Binary trees, BSTs, traversals (DFS/BFS), recursion patterns.

name: Graphs
description: Nodes and edges. BFS, DFS, shortest path, topological sort, union-find.

name: Dynamic Programming
description: Overlapping subproblems and memoization. The category that separates mid from senior.

name: Greedy
description: Locally-optimal choices that lead to globally-optimal solutions. Often deceptively simple.

name: Backtracking
description: Try, recurse, undo. Permutations, combinations, constraint satisfaction.
